subject: [PATCH 2/2] iomap: submit read bio after each extent

reviews.author: Christoph Hellwig
reviews.summary: Approved the patch and suggested folding it into an existing implementation to avoid temporary performance regression and churn.
reviews.raw_body:
On Thu, Jun 25, 2026 at 11:32:40AM -0700, Joanne Koong wrote:
> Yes, that works. I think that's a good idea. fuse only needs
> submit_read logic for readahead. The change would just be:

A nice, I'll fold that in.

> If this fix needs to go into 7.2 though, maybe it makes sense to land
> the v1 implementation [1] + the xfs integrity fix now and do the fuse
> change later?

In a way this is actually simpler as it doesn't require the prototype
change.  And it'll avoid both a temporary fuse performance regression
and a bit of churn.

reviews.author: Christoph Hellwig
reviews.summary: Suggested splitting the patch into a separate submission and landing it in the next kernel version, citing simplicity and avoiding prototype change.
reviews.raw_body:
On Fri, Jun 26, 2026 at 06:33:19AM +0200, Christoph Hellwig wrote:
> On Thu, Jun 25, 2026 at 11:32:40AM -0700, Joanne Koong wrote:
> > Yes, that works. I think that's a good idea. fuse only needs
> > submit_read logic for readahead. The change would just be:
> 
> A nice, I'll fold that in.

Btw, can I get a signoff from you for this?  It looks like splitting
this into a separate patch actually works better.  If you have a
preferred commit log I'd take that as well instead of mine.

This is what I'm currently testing:

https://git.infradead.org/?p=users/hch/misc.git;a=shortlog;h=refs/heads/iomap-read-submit-bio

2026-06-26.patch_summary: Patch to submit read bio after each extent in iomap, with discussion on whether to split it into a separate patch and land it in the next kernel version.

reviews.author: Darrick J. Wong
reviews.summary: Provided a signoff for the patch and suggested a commit log, with no technical comments.

2026-06-25.patch_summary: Patch to submit read bio after each extent in iomap, with discussion on whether to split it into a separate patch and land it in the next kernel version.
